name: Bud's Caeser Salad Dressing (Fjsr05a)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all ingredients except egg substitute well.
2. Let stand at room temperature.
3. Wash 2 large heads of romaine lettuce, removing any debris from the leaves. Spin out excess water and place on a towel in the refrigerator to crisp.
4. Cut 9 slices of sour dough bread and toast both sides. Sprinkle garlic powder on one side and cut into small pieces. Toast garlic croutons in an oven preheated to 300°F (150°C) until crispy, about 25 minutes. Let cool to room temperature.
5. Grate a wedge of Parmesan cheese or cut it into cubes and add to a blender.
6. Assemble: Place cold crisp lettuce in a large bowl. Pour the egg substitute slowly while tossing. When well coated, repeat the process with the dressing. When well coated again, sprinkle the cheese on top. Finally, toss in the croutons.
7. Serve in large salad bowls and garnish with extra Parmesan cheese and fresh ground pepper. Even my teenage kids enjoy this dish. By adding a broiled chicken breast to each serving, along with the remaining sour dough bread, it becomes an entire meal.
